founders.name: TLDR Founders
founders.sub: App defensibility shifts, enterprise token value, and the distribution era.

tldr.name: TLDR
tldr.sub: Nvidia’s AI PCs, Anthropic files to IPO, and search as code generation.

ai.name: TLDR AI
ai.sub: Anthropic IPO filing, OpenAI lands on AWS, and Perplexity reframes search as code.

ITEMS.title: The App Layer Is Dead. Long Live the App Layer
ITEMS.sum: Apps that just wrap a model are easy to replace. The ones that last own a metric the customer cares about and keep making it better.
ITEMS.why: Frames your "defensibility" note from last week — owning a number, not a dashboard.

ITEMS.title: The Distribution Era
ITEMS.sum: The best B2B companies of the next decade build distribution from day 0. The durable advantage has moved to audience.
ITEMS.why: Direct counterpoint to your "build first, sell later" instinct.

ITEMS.title: More Tokens Is Not a Business Outcome
ITEMS.sum: Counting tokens measures effort, not results. Smart buyers use cheap models for easy work and save expensive ones for revenue-tied tasks.
ITEMS.why: Pairs with the enterprise token-value thread you flagged.

ITEMS.title: Headroom — agent context compression
ITEMS.sum: Compresses everything an agent reads before it reaches the model, so users get the same answers at a fraction of the tokens.
ITEMS.why: Open-source, has a skills/ directory — matches your saved tooling.

ITEMS.title: Deliberate — agent decision audit trail
ITEMS.sum: Records every option an agent considers, rejects and executes, and provides an exportable audit trail for compliance teams.

ITEMS.title: The GTM Stack Anthropic Uses, From Its Head of Industries
ITEMS.sum: Eleanor Dorfman walked through Anthropic's GTM stack at SaaStr AI 2026 — the six tools many leading B2B + AI companies share.

ITEMS.title: Nvidia Introduces First PCs Designed for AI Agents
ITEMS.sum: Nvidia unveiled prototype PCs for running AI agents — ~30 laptop and 10 desktop models from Dell, Lenovo, HP, Asus and MSI.
ITEMS.why: Hardware angle on the agent-distribution shift.

ITEMS.title: Anthropic Files to Go Public in Blockbuster Year for IPOs
ITEMS.sum: Anthropic filed confidentially for an IPO that could see it go public this fall. SpaceX and OpenAI are also lining up.
ITEMS.why: You starred all three IPO stories — this is the anchor.

ITEMS.title: Rethinking Search as Code Generation
ITEMS.sum: Perplexity's Search as Code exposes the search stack as SDK primitives a model assembles on demand inside a sandbox — a new cost/performance frontier.
ITEMS.why: Your "search as code gen" highlight — the deep version.

ITEMS.title: The Mathematics of Multi-Tenancy
ITEMS.sum: A bucket migration triggered a swarm of S3 503s. A statistical model for why multi-tenancy works and why S3 won't let you control partitioning.

ITEMS.title: Moderna Gets $50M to Develop mRNA Ebola Vaccine
ITEMS.sum: CEPI pledged $60M+ to accelerate three vaccine candidates against the Bundibugyo ebolavirus, spreading in the DRC.

ITEMS.title: BYD Plans All-Solid-State Batteries for EVs by 2027
ITEMS.sum: Solid-state tech enables 1,000+ km range. First models likely from BYD high-end sub-brands before reaching mass market.

ITEMS.title: The Newest Instagram "Exploit" Is the Goofiest I’ve Seen
ITEMS.sum: Attackers used a VPN near the target, then told Meta support AI the account was hacked — no checks that the recovery email belonged to it.

ITEMS.title: OpenAI and Codex Reach AWS
ITEMS.sum: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are GA on AWS, accessible through existing AWS security, governance and billing workflows.
ITEMS.why: Distribution play — meeting enterprises where their procurement already lives.

ITEMS.title: NVIDIA Releases Nemotron 3 Ultra
ITEMS.sum: 550B params (55B active), the most intelligent US open-weights model — scores 48 on the AA Intelligence Index, ahead of Gemma 4 31B.

ITEMS.title: Qwen3.7-Plus: Multimodal Agent Intelligence
ITEMS.sum: A multimodal agent model that blends GUI and CLI interactions in a single loop, consistent across scaffolds and frameworks.

ITEMS.title: Why Video Agent Models Are Next — Ethan He, xAI Grok Imagine
ITEMS.sum: The lead on Nvidia Cosmos, now at xAI, unpacks what it takes to build frontier image, video and real-time world models.
ITEMS.why: Long read you keep reopening — saved for a flight.

ITEMS.title: Opus 4.8 Part 2: Model Welfare
ITEMS.sum: Anthropic addresses model welfare through studies that mainly rely on self-reporting — and on how hard that is to evaluate truthfully.

ITEMS.title: Running OpenAI Models on Amazon Bedrock
ITEMS.sum: OpenAI cookbook on production workflows on Bedrock: structured outputs, tool calling, file inputs, prompt caching and ops practices.

ITEMS.title: NVIDIA Launches Cosmos 3, Open Frontier Model for Physical AI
ITEMS.sum: A leaderboard-topping open omnimodel with native vision reasoning and generation across text, image, video, sound and action.

ITEMS.title: Anthropic Publishes Its 2026 Economic Index
ITEMS.sum: A data-driven look at which tasks AI is actually being used for across the economy, and how usage has shifted toward coding and analysis.

ITEMS.title: Claude Code Ships Reusable Skills
ITEMS.sum: A library of composable skills agents can load on demand — includes outreach, content and research templates you can fork.

ITEMS.title: Cursor 2.0 Rebuilds Tab Completion
ITEMS.sum: A new speculative-decoding pipeline makes inline suggestions land in under 40ms, with a redesigned multi-file edit flow.

ITEMS.title: Vercel AI SDK 6 Adds First-Class Agents
ITEMS.sum: The SDK now ships an agent runtime with tool-calling, durable steps and streaming UI primitives baked in.

ITEMS.title: Mistral Large 3 Goes Open Weights
ITEMS.sum: Mistral released its flagship under an Apache 2.0 license, betting on adoption over API revenue.

ITEMS.title: Groq Raises $1.5B to Scale Inference
ITEMS.sum: The deterministic-latency chip startup will use the round to build out US data-center capacity ahead of demand.

ITEMS.title: Waymo Opens Freeway Driving to All Riders
ITEMS.sum: After a limited pilot, freeway routes are now live for every rider in three metros, cutting cross-town trips significantly.

ITEMS.title: Figma Ships AI-Native Design Mode
ITEMS.sum: Prompt-to-layout, auto-variants and a redesigned variables system aim to fold generation into the normal design loop.

ITEMS.title: The State of Async Rust in 2026
ITEMS.sum: Where the ecosystem landed after years of churn — what finally stabilized, and the ergonomics that still bite.

ITEMS.title: pgvector 1.0 Lands with HNSW Tuning
ITEMS.sum: Mature index tuning and parallel builds make Postgres a credible default for embedding search at moderate scale.

ITEMS.title: Running 8B Models in the Browser with WebGPU
ITEMS.sum: A walkthrough of quantization and KV-cache tricks that make local, private inference practical on a laptop GPU.

ITEMS.title: Where the AI Margins Actually Are
ITEMS.sum: Inference is becoming a commodity; the durable margin sits in workflow, data gravity and the last mile of integration.

ITEMS.title: Seed Rounds Are Quietly Getting Bigger
ITEMS.sum: Median seed sizes crept up again as funds front-load conviction — with implications for dilution and milestone pressure.

ITEMS.title: Why Dev Tools Have No Moat (and That’s Fine)
ITEMS.sum: Developer tools compete on taste and distribution, not lock-in — and that shapes how you should price and ship.

ITEMS.title: Usage-Based Pricing After the AI Reset
ITEMS.sum: How leading teams are blending seats, usage and outcome pricing now that model costs are real line items.

ITEMS.title: SpaceX Sets Record IPO for Next Week
ITEMS.sum: The offering is expected to be the largest ever, anchoring a blockbuster IPO season alongside Anthropic and OpenAI.

ITEMS.title: EU AI Act Enforcement Begins
ITEMS.sum: The first obligations for high-risk systems take effect, with guidance still landing and penalties phasing in.

ITEMS.title: Commonwealth Fusion Hits Net-Positive Milestone
ITEMS.sum: A sustained burn produced more energy than it consumed, a key step on the path to a demonstration plant.

ITEMS.title: In-Vivo CRISPR Trial Reports Durable Results
ITEMS.sum: A one-time in-body edit held up across the full follow-up window, expanding the case for single-dose genetic therapies.

ITEMS.title: Google’s New Chip Crosses the Error-Correction Threshold
ITEMS.sum: Adding qubits now reduces logical error rates — the long-sought sign that fault-tolerant scaling is on track.

ITEMS.title: A Practical LLM Eval Harness
ITEMS.sum: A lightweight, reproducible harness for grading agents on your own tasks, with a skills directory of graders to adapt.

ITEMS.title: Kafka Drops ZooKeeper for Good
ITEMS.sum: KRaft becomes the only metadata mode, simplifying ops and unlocking faster controller failover at scale.

ITEMS.title: Design Tokens at Scale
ITEMS.sum: Patterns for keeping a token pipeline sane across themes, platforms and a dozen product teams.

ITEMS.title: Long-Term Memory for Agents, Compared
ITEMS.sum: A field guide to the memory architectures shipping in real agents — what each is good at and where they quietly fail.
